task_completion.description: Log in to the GeoServer web interface
task_completion.visual_evidence: GeoServer web interface showing a logged-in state (e.g., 'Welcome admin' or a logout button visible in the top right corner).
GeoServer 'Roles' page showing 'ROLE_DATA_ANALYST' in the list of roles.

task_completion.description: Create the 'analyst_jones' user and ensure it is enabled
task_completion.visual_evidence: GeoServer 'Users' page showing 'analyst_jones' in the list of users, with an indication that the account is enabled (e.g., a green checkmark or 'Enabled' status).
The user properties/edit page for 'analyst_jones' showing 'ROLE_DATA_ANALYST' in the 'Selected' or 'Assigned' roles column.

task_completion.description: Verify the new user's authentication against the GeoServer REST API
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window showing a command (e.g., `curl -u analyst_jones:Analyst2024! -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:8080/geoserver/rest/`) testing authentication against the GeoServer REST API.

task_completion.description: Save the resulting HTTP status code to /home/ga/auth_test_result.txt
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or text editor showing the HTTP status code (e.g., 200) being written to or saved in `/home/ga/auth_test_result.txt`.

integrity.description: The agent used the GeoServer web GUI to create the user and role
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating the GeoServer web interface to add the user and role, rather than using terminal commands to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The REST API test was actually executed and the result captured
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal shows the actual execution of the REST API test command returning a status code, rather than the agent just typing '200' into the text file without testing.

integrity.description: The GeoServer UI shows genuine interaction
integrity.visual_evidence: No signs of HTML manipulation via browser developer tools to fake the presence of the user or role.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected role is 'ROLE_DATA_ANALYST'. The expected user is 'analyst_jones' with password 'Analyst2024!'. The authentication test should target the GeoServer REST API and the expected HTTP status code for successful authentication is 200.
